utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and why is she here ? annual exam . okay . all right . hi , sarah . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so sarah is a 27-year-old female here for her annual visit . so , sarah , how have you been since the last time i saw you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been doing better . um , i've been struggling with my depression , um , a bit more just because we've been trapped really inside and remotely over the past year , so i've been struggling , um , off and on with that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . uh , and from looking at the notes , it looks like we've had you on , uh , prozac 20 milligrams a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: are , are you taking that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i am taking it . i think it's just a lot has been weighing on me lately .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and do you feel like you need an increase in your dose , or do you ... what are you thinking ? do you think that you just need to deal with some stress or you wan na try a , a different , uh , medication or ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think the , the medication has helped me in the past , and maybe just increasing the dose might help me through this patch .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , and what else has been going on with you ? i know that you've had this chronic back pain that we've been dealing with . how's that , how's that going ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i've been managing it . it's still , um , here nor there . just , just keeps , um , it really bothers me when i sit for long periods of time at , at my desk at work . so i have ... it helps when i get up and move , but it gets really stiff and it hurts when i sit down for long periods of time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and do you get any numbing or tingling down your legs or any pain down leg versus the other ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit of numbing , but nothing tingling or hurting down my legs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and does the , um , do those symptoms improve when you stand up or change position ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and any weakness in , in your legs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no weakness , just , just the weird numbing . like , it's , like , almost like it's falling asleep on me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are you able to , um , do your activities of daily living ? do you exercise , go to the store , that type of thing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i am . it bothers me when i'm on my feet for too long and sitting too long , just the extremes of each end .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i know that you've had a coronary artery bypass grafting at the young age of 27 , so how's that going ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i had con- i had a congenital ... you know , i had a congenital artery when i was a baby , so , um , they had to do a cabg on me , um , fairly young in life , but i've been ... my heart's been doing , doing well , and arteries have been looking good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , well , let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam . um , so looking at you , you do n't appear in any distress . um , your neck , there's no thyroid enlargement . uh , your heart i hear a three out of six , systolic ejection murmur , uh , that's stable . your lungs otherwise sound clear . your abdomen is soft , and you do have some pain to palpation of your lumbar spine . uh , and you've had decreased flexion of your back . uh , your lower extremity strength is good , and there's no edema . so let's go ahead and look at some of your results . hey , dragon , show me the ecg . okay , so that looks basically unchanged from last year , which is really good . hey , dragon , show me the lumbar spine x-ray . hey , dragon , show me the back x-ray . great . so this looks good . that's also stable from last year . okay . so let's go ahead and , you know , my , my plan for you at this time , you know , from a chronic back pain standpoint , if you need , um , you know , some more physical therapy , and i can refer you to physical therapy to help with those symptoms that are kind of lingering .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and we can always give you some pain medication if you , if you get some pain periodically with activity . how do you feel about that ? do you need some pain medication ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i think physical therapy is the right way to , way to start out on this .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , order physical therapy referral . and then in terms of your depression , we talked about increasing your prozac , so we'll increase it from 20 milligrams to 40 milligrams . it's just one tablet once a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and i'll send those to your pharmacy . does that sound okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order prozac , 40 milligrams , once a day . and then in terms of your ... the heart bypass that you've had ... let's go ahead and just order another echocardiogram for you , and i wan na continue you on the aspirin for now , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order an echocardiogram . hey , dragon , order aspirin 81 milligrams daily . okay , so the nurse will come in . she'll help you schedule those things , and we'll go from there , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , take care .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 27-year-old female presenting for an annual visit.
Depression
The patient reports struggling with depression off and on over the past year due to being isolated.
The doctor notes the patient is currently prescribed Prozac 20 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking her prescribed Prozac.
The patient confirms she is taking the medication but feels overwhelmed.
The doctor asks if the patient believes she needs a dose increase or a different medication.
The patient suggests that increasing the Prozac dose may be beneficial.
Chronic back pain
The patient reports stiffness and pain in her back when sitting for long periods at work, which improves with movement.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences numbness, tingling, or pain in her legs.
The patient reports mild numbness in her legs but denies tingling or pain.
The doctor asks if the leg symptoms improve with standing or changing positions.
The patient confirms that her symptoms improve when she stands or changes positions.
The doctor asks if the patient has any leg weakness.
The patient denies leg weakness.
Coronary artery bypass grafting
The patient reports a history of CABG due to a congenital artery condition and states her heart is doing well.

Physical exam findings include no distress, supple neck, 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, pain to palpation of the lumbar spine, decreased back flexion, and good lower extremity strength; echocardiogram and lumbar X-rays are stable.
The doctor asks if the patient would like to try pain medication for her back.
The patient declines pain medication and prefers to start with physical therapy.
The doctor orders a physical therapy referral.
The doctor increases the Prozac dose to 40 mg once daily.
The doctor orders an echocardiogram.
The doctor continues aspirin 81 mg daily.
